lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: I am the Chief of Tai Bwo Wannai Village! Who dares set foot in my hut?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a roving adventurer.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Well then adventurer, you would do better to rove elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a travelling explorer.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Well then explorer, you would do better to travel elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a wandering wayfarer.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Well then wayfarer, you would do better to wander elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who me? Oh I'm just a nobody.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Well then NoBody, you would do better to go elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why should I leave?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: This place has been cursed. Our people have angered the gods and have fled the village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this place?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: You ask too many questions. Now begone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are the gods?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: You ask too many questions. Now begone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where have your people fled to?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: You ask too many questions. Now begone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'm going then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trufitus sent me.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Trufitus Shakaya? What news does he send?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trufitus has communed with the gods. Your village was never cursed and your people are free to return.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Bwana, do you speak the truth?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I helped Trufitus gather ingredients for a commune potion.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Then it must be true.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: I cannot thank you enough for what you have done so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your gratitude is all I deserve.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: No, Bwana, you deserve much more.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: I now ask you to do one last thing for our village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is that?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: I ask you to deliver the good news to my three sons Tamayu, Tinsay & Tiadeche, and then return them to the village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Thank you very much. I shall reward you greatly if you succeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I succeed? How hard can it be?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: We shall see, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've had quite enough of the jungle already.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Very well. Return when you are ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So far??

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: I now ask you to do one last thing for our village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: It should be clear to anyone that I am the chief of Tai Bwo Wannai. I ensure that things run smoothly in this village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything interesting to do here?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Yes Bwana, plenty of interesting things to do. Firstly you could make an effort to tidy the place up a little. Equip yourself with a machete and start cutting down the jungle that seems to be growing all around here.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Go and have a chat with Murcaily for information, he's tending the hardwood grove to the east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been doing some work around the village.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: That's good Bwana, I would hate to think that you were just being lazy while you're in my kingdom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: If the player refused to start the quest and talks to Timfraku again

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Are you ready to help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: I ask you to deliver the good news to my three sons Tamayu, Tinsay & Tiadeche, and then return them to the village.

sections.title: Talking to Timfraku after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Welcome back, roving adventurer.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Welcome back, travelling explorer.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Welcome back, wandering wayfarer.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Welcome back, Nobody.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about your sons?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: My first son is Tamayu. After him was born Tinsay. And after him was born Tiadeche.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about Tamayu?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Tamayu was a natural born hunter. He killed his first monkey at the age of three!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about Tinsay?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Tinsay was brought up to be a priest. Be careful with him - he has acted strange since the accident.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What accident?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: It involved Jogres. We don't talk about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about Tiadeche?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Tiadeche always wanted to hunt fish. However, he has not had much success.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: He needs to improve his technique - but he does not heed my advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find your sons?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: On our island of Karamja. They will not have fled far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I need?

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Patience, Bwana, patience.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: And good conversation skills.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

sections.title: Attempting to enter any of the brother's huts in Tai Bwo Wannai Village

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I refuse to take your offerings. I must catch this Karambwan myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tiadeche, instead of using your net, try using this vessel.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It has been specially constructed to catch Karambwan. Once they enter it, they cannot return..

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: As you say, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: This vessel is useless! The Karambwan are not even interested in entering it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been told that the Karambwan feed only on the Karambwanji. This will make good bait.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: The Holy Friend? Bwana, it is sacrilege to feed these fish to Karambwan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just try it.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: This attracts many more Karambwan, but they still break free of the net.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tiadeche, instead of using your net, try using this vessel.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It has been specially constructed to catch Karambwan. Once they enter it, they cannot return..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Also, it has been loaded with Karambwanji. The Karambwan will not be able to resist this bait.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: As you say, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Bwana! I cannot believe my eyes!

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I, Tiadeche, who would never amount to anything, have managed to trap a Karambwan!

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Let me offer to you my first catch as a sign of gratitude.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father would be proud.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Hmph ... my father would not care.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speaking of your father, can we make way to the village? He awaits your return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no need for it, Tiadeche.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We must return to the village now.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I cannot return just yet. We witness the change of a turning tide. The secrets of the Karambwan are to be unlocked!

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Before I return, I must ask you to do one thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that is?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Take a Karambwan vessel to my brother Tinsay. He is the academic amongst us. Tell him I have asked him to study it so that we may craft more of its type.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have the vessel back then?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I need this to practice with. Why don't you craft another one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I ... never mind.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I have caught another Karambwan! I think I ought to give it to you.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Have you taken the Karambwan vessel to Tinsay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Did you ask for crafting instructions as I said?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, but I think I've lost them...

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Well go back and ask him to make another copy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. He gave me this.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Thank you so much. You cannot understand what all of this means to me and our people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you return to the village now?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Yes. I will do as I promised.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am glad to hear this. Let us go.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Not yet. I will stay here a while to catch more Karambwan.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I will meet you in the village once you have helped my brothers and spoken to my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Please hurry! Time is of the essence!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Tiadeche?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tiadeche, I bring good news. Trufitus has communed with the gods - your village is once again safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father requests that you return to the village.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I will return only when I have caught a Karambwan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: You don't know? The Karambwan is a species of octopus native to these waters.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: They have a monopoly on its sale in the pirate town of Brimhaven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When will you be finished?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: At this rate, it could take forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't have forever.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Sometimes, oblivion is all I crave...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I just don't know. You can try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you have me do?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Something ... helpful?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you fishing for the Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I'm trying to catch them with a net as they swim by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And ... ?

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: They keep fighting loose of the twining.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more of the Shaikahan.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: A source of indescribable evil. A beast more foul than your dreams could imagine. It hunts the jungle for anything foolish enough to cross its path.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more of the Shaikahan.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: It stands a full man's length in height. Its fore claws are sharp enough to rip through metal. In its eyes glow the embers of death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you been hunting the Shaikahan?

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Since before I was born, stranger. It is my destiny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When will you succeed?

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: When the gods bless me with success. You may join me on my next hunt, but you may not take part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lead the way.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Shaikhan is at present far too agile for me!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I cannot do enough damage with this spear...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: ... and it seems to be healing too quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps later, Tamayu, first son of Timfraku.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could slay the Shaikahan for you.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: You offer to kill the beast for me? Stop this nonsense, lest I kill you first. It is I who must avenge the death of my kin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take me on your next hunt for the Shaikahan.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Very well, but you must not interfere.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I'm not going to skin that! In the jungle, we help only those who help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've noticed you commenting on the Shaikahan's agility. Try drinking this potion.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I am beginning to match Shaikahan's agility!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I cannot do enough damage ...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: ... and it seems to be healing too quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tamayu, try using this weapon.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Thank you Bwana. Now I must prepare for my next hunt.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Shaikhan is at present far too agile for me!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I can do enough damage ...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: ... but it seems to be healing too quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've noticed you commenting on the Shaikahan's agility. Try drinking this potion.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Thank you Bwana. Now I must prepare for my next hunt.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I feel much more agile, but I am not sure whether I can match the Shaikahan...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Thank you Bwana. Now I must prepare for my next hunt.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I feel much more agile, but I am not sure whether I can match the Shaikahan...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Thank you Bwana. Now I must prepare for my next hunt.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I feel much more agile, but I am not sure whether I can match the Shaikahan...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tamayu, I have brought you more agility potion.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Thank you Bwana. Now I must prepare for my next hunt.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I feel agile enough to finally match the Shaikahan!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I have matched the Shaikahan in agility! He can no longer avoid my strikes!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I can do enough damage ...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: ... but it seems to be healing too quickly!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I have matched the Shaikahan in agility! He can no longer avoid my strikes!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I can do enough damage ...

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: ... and it does seem quite sick! Perhaps another poison would be more effective?

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: The end approaches, Shaikahan!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Gods! Witness this triumph!

sections.title: Talking to Tamayu after his triumph against the Shaikahan

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I did it! I, Tamayu, first son of Timfraku, did slay the Shaikahan!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: The deaths of my kin have been avenged. You are my witness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Worry not - I am your witness. Shall we return to the village now?

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Yes. Go on ahead without me. I have further matters to attend to here.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I will meet you in the village once you have helped my brothers and spoken to my father.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Let me guess - you want me to skin that monkey. Very well: I shall, since you have assisted me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tamayu, first son of Timfraku!

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: What do you want, stranger?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tamayu, I bring good news. Trufitus has communed with the gods - your village is once again safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father requests that you return to the village.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Then know this, stranger.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I vow upon the blood of my fallen kin that I shall not set foot in that godforsaken village till I have slain the Shaikahan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could slay it after we return to the village.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: No! Your pitiful whining tires me. Now let me prepare.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You again! I thought I told you to go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did. I came back.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: So I see. What would a fool like you want with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I am Lubufu, son of Kedoaba, brother of Kedenda and second cousin twice removed of Ibeli!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Kedoaba?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Kedoaba is my father, the father of Kedenda and second uncle twice removed of Ibeli!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Kedenda?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Kedenda is the son of my father, my brother and second cousin twice removed of Ibeli!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Ibeli?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Ibeli is the second nephew twice removed of my father, and the second cousin twice removed of myself and my brother!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any children?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Alas, no. Focussed and determined in my trade as I was, I never found a suitable wife.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: It is a great shame that the secret of the Karambwan has to die with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could tell me the secret...

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Hah! What a stupid thought! The secret is mine and mine alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Our island of Karamja. Long ago, before you were even a thought in the back of a mind, I used to play on these beaches with the pebbles and stones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't have many friends then?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Silence!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: As I grew older the ways of the world became apparent to me, and my attention turned from the rocks of the land to the fish in the sea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And ... ?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Despite what you might hear, there are plenty of fish in the sea! Especially when they can only be caught by me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Nowadays I mostly fish for Karambwan and collect their bait. Collecting the bait has become increasingly tiresome, what with my age and health.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How old are you?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: An order of magnitude older than you! What an impudent question! Collecting bait has become hard enough at this age as it is without having to endure the chiding of you young people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which other young people come by here?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Hmm ... nobody I can recall right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you use for bait?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: That is part of the secret! Why would I tell you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could help collect the bait.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: And why would you help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound like you could do with the help.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well perhaps you're right. It's nigh time I could do with some help after all.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I have a fair amount of bait left over since last time I collected some, but I will be needing more shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Where can I find the bait? I don't even know what it is!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Patience. I have kept the nature of the Karambwan a closely guarded secret. There is nobody alive who knows more about them than I do. Furthermore, there is nobody alive who even knows how they feed.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You must therefore understand that what I am about to tell you is given in complete trust, and that you are to tell nobody else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Very well. The Karambwan are a form of octopus native to our island of Karamja. They feed solely on Karambwanji, a brightly coloured fish which also inhabits the coastal waters surrounding the island.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Karambwanji congregate in coastal shoals near the Holy Lake. You will find it easiest to fish for them there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the Holy Lake?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Just to the south of Tai Bwo Wannai Village. I'm sure you've met the locals already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what do I use to fish for the bait?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Any small net will do. Return here as soon as you have caught 20 Karambwanji.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound like you need help.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: How dare you say such a thing! Be off with you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I enjoy collecting meaningless items for people.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Meaningless? You know nothing of the Karambwan! Now leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right. I don't want to help.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I didn't for one moment think you did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: A species of octopus native to the island which I and I alone know how to catch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do with your Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I eat what I need and sell the rest to the Shrimp and Parrot restaurant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the Shrimp and Parrot?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You don't know anything do you? Just north of here, in Brimhaven!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you use for bait?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: That is part of the secret! Why would I tell you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does Karambwan taste like?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: That depends entirely on how it is cooked...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Novicely cooked Karambwan is often poisonous to large mammals such as humans. If you buy some and cook it, be careful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come for your soul.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well you've wasted your time. My soul is long gone, along with my compassion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm from the Fishing Guild Anti-Monopoly Department.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: And I'm from the Island Karamja. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We demand that you hand over the secret of the Karambwan.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Erm ... no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, ok then. It was worth a try.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any spare monkey skin lying around?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Funny you should say that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why's that? Do you have any?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well, people have been asking me for monkey skins all day!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: But no, I'm out of monkey skin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard something about an accident.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You will want to ask one of the tribesmen. I don't usually talk about it.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Have you retrieved the Karambwanji yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have some here.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Thank you! This should tide me over for the next month.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Hurry along now. I still need [#] more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still fishing for them.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well hurry! My reserves are beginning to run low.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Have you retrieved the Karambwanji yet?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I still need [#] more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have some here.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Thank you! This should tide me over for the next month.

sections.title: Talking to Lubufu after delivering raw Karambwanji

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: A species of octopus native to the island which I and I alone know how to catch.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You should really ought to know that by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you use to catch Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well ... since you ask, a specially constructed octopus pot. But don't tell anybody - that's part of the secret!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: My, you're keen aren't you?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: How would you feel about becoming my apprentice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a slight change in attitude...

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: As much as I need to protect the secret, I should not carry it to the grave.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You seem a trustworthy type of person. Do you want to train as my apprentice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I'm glad to hear it. I'm sure the secrets of the Karambwan will be safe with you.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: In our language, Karambwan literally means 'Friend of Karamja.' As a friend of mine I will now show you how to fish for them.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: There are two aspects to fishing for Karambwan. Firstly, you must have the correct bait. Secondly, you must have the correct equipment.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You already know of the Karambwanji, which shoal in the Holy Lake to the south. As I have explained, the Karambwan feed only on the Karambwanji, which are therefore the perfect bait.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what kind of equipment do you use?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: It is futile to attempt to catch Karambwan with something as basic as a net. They are too strong and vicious to be caught in such a fashion.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I have spent many years designing a specially shaped vessel, into which Karambwan may pass but from which they may not return.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: If this vessel is loaded with Karambwanji, and then repeatedly lowered into a Karambwan shoal, then you should shortly find a Karambwan octopus trapped inside.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I will now give you a Karambwan fishing vessel with which you can practise. Make sure you do not lose it. Under no circumstance may you give or show this to anybody. Do you understand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I understand.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Feel free to practise on my fishing spot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well the offer remains open. You're the most trustworthy person I have met in a long time.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Let me know if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: How fares my apprentice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine thanks.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: And I'm glad to hear it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been catching a whole load of Karambwan!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Look at you - outgrowing your boots already!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've lost my Karambwan vessel.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: You did what!? I hope nobody else has seen it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry - nobody will find it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... a shark ate it!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well you'd better hope for your sake that this shark is never caught!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... a Karambwan stole it!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well you'd better hope for your sake that this Karambwan is never caught!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... it floated away!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well you'd better hope for your sake that it never floats back!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: In the meantime, you can practise with this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Don't mention it. I mean that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awful!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everybody keeps asking me to run around and do their errands!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Living the hard life of a fisherman now, eh.

sections.title: Talking to Lubufu after refusing to be his apprentice

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Ah ... you again. Have you changed your mind yet? Do you want to train as my apprentice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well the offer remains open. You're the most trustworthy person I have met in a long time.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Let me know if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Watch where you're going, young whippersnapper!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wasn't going anywhere...

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: Well then go away from here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a whippersnapper?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: It's a whip. Which snaps. Like me. Now leave!

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: I am Lubufu - the only fisherman who knows the secret of the Karambwan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Lubufu
lines.text: What a foolish question! Now leave!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Braaar! Tinsay's the name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tinsay, I bring good news. Trufitus has communed with the gods - your village is once again safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father requests that you return to the village.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I will return only once I have repaired the tribal statue. Bones!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the tribal statue?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Just near my father's hut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that's in the village!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I'm ... er ... collecting materials to repair the statue with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I suppose you want me to fetch them for you?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Good idea! Yes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean you were just sitting here waiting for people to bring you things you need?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Yes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you don't see anything wrong with that?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right ... what do you need?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Hmm ... I think I need banana in Karamjan rum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And you're going to use this to repair the statue?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: But of course! Bwahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your brother Tiadeche has sent me. He asks you to study this vessel and produce crafting instructions.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You first help me, then I'll help you.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Have you found my banana in Karamjan rum yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Well, hand it here then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm ... I think I lost it in the wash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh ... I think I left it in my other trousers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, my pet monkey ate it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I can't use this - there is a banana blocking the bottle!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Find some way of actually getting the banana inside the bottle.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Yes ... that's it! Hits just the spot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? I thought you needed that for tribal statue repair!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I did!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we return to the village now?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Not yet. I need one more thing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I'm thinking...

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Seaweed ... Yes - a seaweed sandwich will do the trick.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Aha! A seaweed in monkey skin sandwich!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this really necessary?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Yes. Essential.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Karamjan rum?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: It's available in any respectable drinking establishment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find bananas?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: They grow everywhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I convince you to return to the village?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Oblige me by fetching my drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just how insane are you?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: On a scale of 1 to barmy , I'm about [#].

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: On a scale of 1 to kooky , I'm about [#].

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: On a scale of 1 to mad , I'm about [#].

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: On a scale of 1 to loopy , I'm about [#].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard something about an accident.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Maurhah!! Jooooogre bones!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Ahem. Sorry. We don't usually talk about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still looking.

sections.title: Talking to Tinsay again with his second request

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Have you found my seaweed in monkey skin sandwich yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Well, hand it here then.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Yes ... perfect! You really do not understand how necessary that was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we go now?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Erm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Say something!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that mean you're not coming or not saying anything?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I'm not coming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I need one more thing... I need ... Jogre bones ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jogre bones? Why do you keep-

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Yes ... burnt Jogre bones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... burnt Jogre bones?? How am I-

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Indeed. Burnt Jogre bones marinated in... Marinated in...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Marinated???

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Marinated in Karambwanji!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Mmm ... Yes ... That will be good ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're just going to eat it, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: No! Burnt Jogre bones marinated in Karambwanji are in fact essential in the trade of tribal statue repair!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back ... eventually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I can't use this - there is far too much monkey flesh in it.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Try to find some way of removing the monkey flesh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh ... I think I left it in my other trousers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still looking.

sections.title: Talking to Tinsay again with his third request

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Have you found my burnt Jogre bones marinated in Karambwanji yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Well, hand it here then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I think this needs a bit of Karambwanji. They also don't seem quite burnt enough.}}

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: That was awful! The Jogre bones were not nearly burnt enough.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: And the Jogre bones were hardly even marinated!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You'll have to make it again. Remember to burn the Jogre bones!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: That was awful! The Jogre bones were not nearly burnt enough.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: And the Jogre bones were hardly even marinated!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You'll have to make it again. Remember to burn the Jogre bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I think this needs a bit of Karambwanji.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: That was awful! The Jogre bones were hardly even marinated!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You'll have to make it again. Remember to marinate the Jogre bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: That was awful! The Jogre bones were not nearly burnt enough.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You'll have to make it again. Remember to burn the Jogre bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: That was awful! The Jogre bones were not nearly burnt enough.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You'll have to make it again. Remember to burn the Jogre bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I burn Jogre bones?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You must either be very skilled or find a way to make them super hot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I marinate something?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: You cook it in a sauce, obviously.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Karambwanji?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: They're fish. Do I look like a fisherman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I end this silly quest?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Patience, Bwana, patience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just how insane are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard something about an accident.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Maurhah!! Jooooogre bones!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Ahem. Sorry. We don't usually talk about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Choke!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gulp!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gasp!

sections.title: Giving Tinsay the perfect Jogre bones

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Have you found my burnt Jogre bones marinated in Karambwanji yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it right here.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Finally! A near lifetime of craving satisfied!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assume you're now able to repair the statue...

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Perfectly able, my friend, thanks to your ministrations!

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Probably best you don't go cooking any more Karambwanji from now on, they can be a bit volatile!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you return to the village now?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Must I ... ?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I insist - and so does your father.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: He will not be pleased with me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: It's complicated - we don't really talk about it.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Very well. I will meet you in the village once you have helped my brothers and spoken to my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you return to the village now?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Must I ... ?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your brother Tiadeche has sent me. He asks you to study this vessel and produce crafting instructions.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Very well - hand it over.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: I hope that's what he wanted.

sections.title: Talking to Timfraku after getting all three sons home

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: My sons have returned to the village!

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Roving adventurer, please accept my eternal gratitude!

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Travelling explorer, please accept my eternal gratitude!

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Wandering wayfarer, please accept my eternal gratitude!

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Nobody, please accept my eternal gratitude!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh it was nothing really.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: It was? Very well. I still would that you accept my reward.}}

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather have some gold please.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Of course. That is in your nature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know your sons have serious issues...

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Of course. I sometimes wonder if they know they have issues. Anyway, please accept also my reward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eternal gratitude accepted.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: Very good. The spirits of my family will watch over you. And now, I would that you also accept a small token of my appreciation.

lines.speaker: Timfraku
lines.text: You should go and see my sons in the village. I think they have something for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure Tinsay said to use Karambwanji paste, not Karambwan paste...

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Hello, Bwana! We three have finally returned to the village!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I see.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I wish to reward you in return for helping me.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: I have been attempting to cook the Karambwan. As we all know, they are poisonous if not cooked properly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. It's how I, or rather Tamayu, killed the Shaikahan.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Indeed. I have determined precisely how long to cook them for so that they are edible.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Thanks to you, Lubufu's Karambwan monopoly is over. If you ever need some, then come to me and not the store in Brimhaven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Tiadeche.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Hello, Bwana! Would you like to buy some Karambwan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Tiadeche
lines.text: Very well. Let me know if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Braaar! Your meals are truly excellent, Bwana ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad to hear it.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: ... but there are some areas which could use some ... improvement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Such as?

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Let me show you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Oh, and before you go: I finally got around to repairing that statue. You might want to take a look. ... Jogre bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Tinsay.

lines.speaker: Tinsay
lines.text: Why hello there! What are you doing back here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just passing through.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Welcome back to Tai Bwo Wannai, stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I no longer feel a stranger here.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: And neither do we, thanks to you. My father has requested I give you something of value.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: I have not much of worth to give, apart from my fighting technique.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: This is the one thing I can give. My family have honoured me with this spear. But the honour is truly yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Take care, Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Tamayu, first son of Timfraku.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Stranger, why you have returned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just passing through.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: Using your new found poison I have been creating spears. Would you like to buy some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Tamayu
lines.text: As you wish, stranger.
